merge.diff:
diff --git a/src/click/core.py b/src/click/core.py
index f57ada6..470233b 100644
--- a/src/click/core.py
+++ b/src/click/core.py
@@ -15,6 +15,7 @@ from contextlib import ExitStack
 from functools import update_wrapper
 from gettext import gettext as _
 from gettext import ngettext
+from itertools import islice
 from itertools import repeat
 from types import TracebackType
 
@@ -93,6 +94,18 @@ def batch(iterable: cabc.Iterable[V], batch_size: int) -> list[tuple[V, ...]]:
     return list(zip(*repeat(iter(iterable), batch_size), strict=False))
 
 
+def _batch_values(
+    values: cabc.Iterable[V], batch_size: int
+) -> cabc.Iterator[tuple[V, ...]]:
+    """Group ``values`` into tuples of at most ``batch_size`` items,
+    preserving a final partial batch instead of dropping it.
+    """
+    it = iter(values)
+
+    while chunk := tuple(islice(it, batch_size)):
+        yield chunk
+
+
 @contextmanager
 def augment_usage_errors(
     ctx: Context, param: Parameter | None = None
@@ -1196,6 +1209,9 @@ class Command:
         for param in iter_params_for_processing(param_order, self.get_params(ctx)):
             value, args = param.handle_parse_result(ctx, opts, args)
 
+        if not ctx.resilient_parsing:
+            self._resolve_dynamic_defaults(ctx)
+
         if args and not ctx.allow_extra_args and not ctx.resilient_parsing:
             ctx.fail(
                 ngettext(
@@ -1209,6 +1225,31 @@ class Command:
         ctx._opt_prefixes.update(parser._opt_prefixes)
         return args
 
+    def _resolve_dynamic_defaults(self, ctx: Context) -> None:
+        """Second-pass resolution for options with dictionary-based
+        dynamic defaults. Runs after every parameter has consumed its
+        value from the command line, environment variables, and
+        configuration files, so referenced option values are final.
+
+        Only applies to options whose value source is still
+        :attr:`ParameterSource.DEFAULT`, i.e. no explicit value was
+        provided anywhere else.
+        """
+        for param in self.get_params(ctx):
+            if (
+                isinstance(param, Option)
+                and param._dynamic_default is not None
+                and ctx.get_parameter_source(param.name)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
+                is ParameterSource.DEFAULT
+            ):
+                resolved = param.resolve_dynamic_default(ctx)
+
+                if resolved is not None:
+                    value = param.process_value(ctx, resolved)
+
+                    if param.expose_value:
+                        ctx.params[param.name] = value  # type: ignore[index]
+
     def invoke(self, ctx: Context) -> t.Any:
         """Given a context, this invokes the attached callback (if it exists)
         in the right way.
@@ -2506,6 +2547,10 @@ class Option(Parameter):
                                context.
     :param help: the help string.
     :param hidden: hide this option from help outputs.
+    :param batch_size: if ``multiple`` is set, process the collected
+                       values in chunks of at most this many items
+                       instead of all at once. Must not be negative,
+                       and is only valid when ``multiple`` is ``True``.
     :param attrs: Other command arguments described in :class:`Parameter`.
 
     .. versionchanged:: 8.2
@@ -2549,6 +2594,7 @@ class Option(Parameter):
         show_choices: bool = True,
         show_envvar: bool = False,
         deprecated: bool | str = False,
+        batch_size: int | None = None,
         **attrs: t.Any,
     ) -> None:
         if help:
@@ -2636,6 +2682,22 @@ class Option(Parameter):
         self.show_default = show_default
         self.show_choices = show_choices
         self.show_envvar = show_envvar
+        self.batch_size = batch_size
+
+        if batch_size is not None and batch_size < 0:
+            raise ValueError("'batch_size' must not be negative.")
+
+        self._dynamic_default: dict[str, dict[str, t.Any]] | None = None
+
+        if isinstance(self.default, dict):
+            for key, mapping in self.default.items():
+                if not isinstance(key, str):
+                    raise TypeError("Dynamic default keys must be strings")
+                if not isinstance(mapping, dict):
+                    raise TypeError("Dynamic default values must be dictionaries")
+
+            self._dynamic_default = self.default
+            self.default = None
 
         if __debug__:
             if deprecated and prompt:
@@ -2662,6 +2724,9 @@ class Option(Parameter):
                 if self.is_flag:
                     raise TypeError("'count' is not valid with 'is_flag'.")
 
+            if self.batch_size is not None and not self.multiple:
+                raise TypeError("'batch_size' is only valid with 'multiple'.")
+
     def to_info_dict(self) -> dict[str, t.Any]:
         info_dict = super().to_info_dict()
         info_dict.update(
@@ -2671,9 +2736,18 @@ class Option
